claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for your one child.

Let me ask you a few questions:

**What is your child's name and how old are they?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex and they're 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Since Alex is 17 years old, their eligibility depends on whether they're in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, studying at school, college, or in approved training courses)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex isn't in school or college anymore, but they've just started an apprenticeship.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to clarify one more detail:

**Is Alex's apprenticeship in England?**

This is important because the rules specifically state that Child Benefit stops if a child starts an apprenticeship in England.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, the apprenticeship is in England.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have one more important question to complete my assessment:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Eligibility Decision

I've completed my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility. Unfortunately, **you are not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Here's why:**

Although Alex is 17 years old (under 20), and Child Benefit can normally continue for children aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training, **apprenticeships in England are specifically excluded** from this provision. 

According to UK Child Benefit rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. Since Alex has just started an apprenticeship in England, your Child Benefit entitlement for Alex will end.

If you were previously receiving Child Benefit for Alex, you should inform HMRC about this change in circumstances so they can stop your payments accordingly.

I'm sorry this isn't the outcome you were hoping for. Is there anything else I can help clarify?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the Child Benefit rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. While Child Benefit can normally continue for children aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training, apprenticeships in England are specifically excluded from this provision. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and has one child, Alex, who is 17 years old. Although Alex is under 20, they have recently started an apprenticeship in England. Under UK Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, even if they are still under the age of 20. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

According to the Child Benefit rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. While Child Benefit can normally continue for children aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training, apprenticeships in England are specifically excluded from this provision. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and has one child, Alex, who is 17 years old. Although Alex is under 20, they have recently started an apprenticeship in England. Under UK Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, even if they are still under the age of 20. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
